content.description:
Transclusion embeds one item's content inside another, creating a live reference that updates when the source changes. This enables documentation to stay in sync and knowledge to be composed from atomic pieces.

## Syntax

Transclusion uses markdown image syntax with `item://` URLs:

| Syntax | Effect |
|--------|--------|
| `[Text](item://id)` | Link (click to navigate) |
| `![Text](item://id)` | Transclude (embed content) |

The `!` prefix (like markdown images) means "embed this here."

## Full Transclusion

```markdown
![My Note](item://note-uuid)
```

Renders the entire item using its view, wrapped in a transclusion container with a header linking to the source.

## Partial Transclusion

Embed just a specific field or section:

### By Field

```markdown
![](item://id#description)     Transclude content.description
![](item://id#code)            Transclude content.code
![](item://id#name)            Transclude the name field
```

### By Named Region

Code and markdown can define named regions:

```javascript
// [BEGIN:myFunction]
function myFunction() {
  return 42;
}
// [END:myFunction]
```

Transclude just that region:

```markdown
![](item://id#code?region=myFunction)
```

### By Line Range

```markdown
![](item://id#code?lines=10-20)    Lines 10-20
![](item://id#code?lines=5)        Just line 5
![](item://id#code?lines=1-50)     First 50 lines
```

### Combined

```markdown
![](item://id#code?region=myFunction&lines=1-5)
```

First extracts the region, then applies the line range within it.

## Query Blocks

For dynamic content, use query blocks:

````markdown
```query
const notes = await api.query(i => i.type === 'note-type-uuid');
return notes.map(n => `- [${n.name}](item://${n.id})`).join('\n');
```
````

The JavaScript executes at render time, producing markdown that gets rendered.

### Query Helpers

Query blocks have access to `transclude()` and `transcludeEach()`:

```javascript
const items = await api.query(...);
return transcludeEach(items, item => 
  `- **${item.name}**: ${item.content?.description?.split('\n')[0]}`
);
```

## How It Works

1. [hobson-markdown](item://a1b2c3d4-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-e1f2a3b4c5d6) parses markdown
2. Finds `![...](item://...)` patterns
3. Fetches the referenced item
4. For full transclusion: calls `api.renderItem()`
5. For partial: extracts field/region/lines and displays as code block
6. Wraps in container with "Transcluded from:" header

## Transclusion vs Attachments

| Aspect | Transclusion | [Attachments](item://c0c0c0c0-0005-0000-0000-000000000000) |
|--------|-------------|----------|
| Syntax | `![](item://id)` in markdown | `attachments` array in item |
| Display | Inline within text flow | As positioned windows |
| Relationship | Implicit via content | Explicit in structure |
| Use case | Documentation, composition | Spatial organization |

An item can be both transcluded AND a child—these are orthogonal.

## Visual Distinction

Transcluded content appears in a box with:
- Light gray background
- Border
- "Transcluded from: [Item Name]" header
- Clicking the header navigates to the source

## See Also

- [hobson-markdown](item://a1b2c3d4-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-e1f2a3b4c5d6) — The library implementing transclusion
- [concept:navigation](item://c0c0c0c0-0003-0000-0000-000000000000) — Links vs embeds
- [concept:attachments](item://c0c0c0c0-0005-0000-0000-000000000000) — Structural relationships
- [concept:repl](item://c0c0c0c0-0008-0000-0000-000000000000) — Testing query blocks interactively
